description_long: This is a three-story residential house with a gray shingle exterior and a dark gabled roof. The front facade features a covered porch with white railings, steps, and a circular window above the entryway, flanked by two rectangular windows on each upper floor. The rear side of the house displays a symmetrical arrangement of large rectangular windows across all three stories, providing ample natural light. The side walls are mostly plain, with one side having a pair of small windows and the other side being windowless. The house sits on a concrete foundation and the porch is supported by columns. The overall design is simple and functional, with a focus on verticality and straightforward geometry.
description: A three-story gray shingle house with a gabled roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangements. The design is vertical and functional, with a prominent entryway and large rear windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing a plain shingle wall with two small windows and a partial view of the front porch.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the covered porch, circular window, entry steps, and two rows of rectangular windows above.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a completely plain shingle wall and a partial view of the porch.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying three rows of large, evenly spaced rectangular windows across all stories.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front and rear, minimal side windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, white railings, columns, and a circular accent window above the main door
